name: id-b-serial-protocol
claim: Belief ids are opaque b-prefixed serials: the prefix carries no type, grade, or vocabulary semantics, and identity encodes nothing derivable from the node - the a/c prefixes this replaces encoded a retired type name and a derived grade, and rotted when the vocabulary moved. The 2026-07-02 migration letter-swapped the cb: graph's legacy a/c ids to b-serials, serials preserved, collision-free because the a and c serial ranges were disjoint (a098-a565, c026-c067); every pre-migration reference therefore resolves by the same swap, applied as a resolution fallback that exact matches always beat. This was the last alpha-rename: it was sound only because b-space was virgin and no external consumer held references, conditions the migration itself ended.
rules: New belief ids are minted from the single b-series: bare local id b<serial>, zero-padded to three digits below 1000, the serial continuing past the maximum across a-, b-, and c-prefixed ids so serials stay unique within the letter-swap alias space.
The id prefix does no load-bearing work: no code path detects type, contract-grade, or any other node property by id prefix - the b056 prohibition, extended from contract-grade to every property.

Legacy ids resolve through the letter-swap alias ([ac]<serial> -> b<serial>) strictly as a fallback after exact match fails, so collections still carrying real a/c ids are never re-pointed by the alias.

Historical artifacts keep their legacy spellings: artifact-URI slugs, proposal names, commit messages, and archived documents (chronicles/, positions/, plans/, nursery/) are not renamed; the alias keeps them resolvable.
Graph-wide id renames are closed: identity outlives vocabulary, and no future migration may re-spell existing ids.
invariants: For all beliefs in the cb: graph: the local id matches b<digits>.
For all pairs of cb: ids: serial numbers are unique regardless of historical prefix letter, so the letter-swap alias never has two candidate targets.

evidence.detail: Migration executed per plans/cb-id-b-migration/design.md: all 226 cb: ids letter-swapped in beliefs.json/todos.json plus the living docs, with the legacy alias, single-series minting, and the deletion of the c-prefix contract heuristics landed in code first (407 tests, schema verify 20/20, commit provenance green on both sides of the sweep). codepath:/cb-okf:/belief-collections stay on legacy ids pending per-collection plans - their a/c serials collide, so the pure letter-swap does not extend to them.
